name: Bill Clinton
said: I had no knowledge of his crimes.
I took four trips on his plane.
did: Flight logs show 26+ flights on Epstein's planes — not 4 as claimed.
Visited Epstein's private island — confirmed by witness testimony.
Secret Service detail reportedly declined on some Epstein flights.
facts: FAA flight logs: 26+ flights documented on Epstein's aircraft.
Clinton initially claimed 4 trips — logs show far more.
Witness testimony places Clinton on Little St. James island.
No criminal charges filed.
Photos with Epstein and Maxwell at multiple events.

name: Alan Dershowitz
said: I never had sex with any underage person.
did: Named by Virginia Giuffre as someone she was trafficked to — under oath.
Helped negotiate Epstein's 2008 sweetheart plea deal as part of legal team.
Settled defamation claims with Giuffre (2024) — then recanted his denial.
Admitted getting a massage at Epstein's mansion — 'I kept my underwear on.'
facts: Named in Giuffre sworn testimony as person she was directed to have sex with as a minor.
Part of Epstein's legal defense team that negotiated 2008 NPA.
Settled with Giuffre 2024 — retracted years of calling her a liar.
After settlement, stated he had 'made a mistake' in his denials.
Frequent visitor to Epstein properties — self-admitted.

name: Bill Gates
said: I didn't have any business relationship with him.
did: Met with Epstein multiple times AFTER 2008 conviction — NYT documented.
Flew on Epstein's plane at least once (2013).
Epstein advised Gates on philanthropy and marriage.
Melinda Gates cited Epstein meetings as factor in divorce.
facts: NYT: Gates met Epstein 'numerous times' starting 2011 — after conviction.
Gates Foundation: Epstein facilitated at least one multimillion-dollar donation.
Email from Gates to colleagues: 'His lifestyle is very different and kind of intriguing.'
Melinda French Gates: Epstein meetings were one factor in filing for divorce.
No allegations of criminal conduct against Gates.
Gates initially minimized contact — later acknowledged multiple meetings.

name: Leon Black
role: CEO, Apollo Global Management / Epstein Associate
said: I was not involved in any wrongdoing.
did: Paid Epstein $158 million between 2012-2017 — AFTER conviction.
Payments described as 'professional services' — nature unclear.
Stepped down as Apollo CEO after payments revealed (2021).
Guzel Ganieva accused Black of sexual assault — settled.
facts: Apollo review (Dechert LLP): confirmed $158M in payments to Epstein 2012-2017.
Payments were AFTER Epstein's 2008 conviction for sex trafficking.
Black stepped down as CEO March 2021 — remained chairman briefly.
Ganieva lawsuit: alleged rape and sexual abuse — settled out of court.
$158M for 'tax and estate planning services' — questioned by investors and media.
sources: Dechert LLP independent review for Apollo

name: Jes Staley

facts: FCA/PRA investigation: Staley 'mischaracterized' Epstein relationship to regulators.

name: JP Morgan Chase

Internal compliance flagged Epstein — management overrode warnings.
facts: Epstein client 2003-2018 — maintained relationship 10 years after sex trafficking conviction.
$290M settlement with Epstein victims (SDNY, 2023).
$75M settlement with US Virgin Islands.
Internal emails show compliance raised concerns — overridden by senior management.
Jes Staley facilitated the continued relationship (per JP Morgan's own lawsuit against him).

name: Deutsche Bank
did: Accepted Epstein as client in 2013 — 5 years AFTER conviction.
Processed hundreds of transactions for Epstein.
Paid $150M fine to NY regulators for compliance failures.
Fined for facilitating suspicious transactions including payments to known associates.
facts: NYDFS: $150M penalty for 'significant compliance failures' in Epstein account (July 2020).
Onboarded Epstein 2013 — after conviction, after JP Morgan dropped him.
Processed transactions including 'payments to individuals who have been publicly identified as co-conspirators.'
Internal red flags ignored or overridden.
